Title: A MYB61-SWB9-KOs module regulates grain chalkiness via gibberellin biosynthesis in rice endosperm

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in all examined tissues; highly expressed in stem and seed endosperm at 25 DAF
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endosperm, especially developing endosperm; also stem
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Bioactive gibberellins (GA1; GA3; GA4; GA7) affecting grain chalkiness
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls endosperm starch synthesis/amylopectin chain-length distribution and thereby grain appearance quality/chalkiness
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Kinesin-4 protein with conserved ATPase domain and coiled-coil domain; new allele of BC12/GDD1/MTD1
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SWB9 increases endosperm gibberellin accumulation, elevates chalky grain rate and chalkiness degree, alters amylopectin chain-length distribution, reduces amylose, and causes white-belly endosperm
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: MutMap/map-based cloning; mutant characterization; complementation; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; luciferase transactivation assay; EMSA; endogenous GA quantification; starch physicochemical analysis; RNA-seq
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of 15 DAF endosperm (platform DNBSEQ-T7/Gene+Seq-2000); MutMap whole-genome sequencing on Illumina HiSeq PE150; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing endosperm stage; exogenous GA3 treatment; organ-specific comparison between endosperm and leaf
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced plant height, panicle length, primary branch number, grain length, grain thickness, thousand-grain weight; increased tiller number and delayed heading
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a transcriptional inhibitor despite being a kinesin; negatively regulates KO genes in endosperm but GA-biosynthetic genes are mostly reduced in leaf, indicating organ-specific regulation; partially rescued by SWB9 overexpression in myb61 background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SWB9 was identified as the causal gene for the swb9 white-belly mutant. 2. SWB9 directly binds promoters of KO1, KO2 and KOL5 and represses their transcription in endosperm. 3. Loss of SWB9 elevates endogenous GA levels in endosperm, disrupts starch structure, increases short amylopectin chains, and causes chalkiness. 4. SWB9 functions downstream of MYB61 and can partially rescue myb61 grain phenotypes when overexpressed.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A target for improving rice grain appearance quality by tuning endosperm GA homeostasis; caution needed because complete loss causes multiple agronomic penalties, so fine-tuning or endosperm-specific engineering is preferable
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for transcription/localization assays; native SWB9 promoter for complementation; pCUbi1390 ubiquitin promoter for overexpression lines

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation of ent-kaurene to ent-kaurenoic acid in an early gibberellin biosynthetic step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies direct precursor toward bioactive gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early control step suggested in this study; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in swb9 endosperm; induced under high GA3 treatment; directly repressed by SWB9
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects endosperm starch synthesis and rice grain chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: KO1 overexpression increases endogenous GAs and increases chalky grain rate/degree; ko1 mutant reduces GA1 and GA3 to very low/undetectable levels in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; endogenous GA quantification; luciferase assay; EMSA; grain quality phenotyping; amylopectin chain-length analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increases grain chalkiness and shifts amylopectin toward more short chains
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter directly bound and transcriptionally repressed by SWB9 in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KO1 is a direct downstream target of SWB9. 2. KO1 overexpression elevates endosperm GA content and increases chalkiness. 3. ko1 lowers bioactive GA levels in endosperm, supporting its role in GA biosynthesis linked to grain quality.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Downregulating KO1 in endosperm may reduce excessive GA accumulation and chalkiness; excessive overexpression is undesirable for grain appearance
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCUbi1390 ubiquitin promoter for overexpression; native promoter used in LUC assays

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation of ent-kaurene to ent-kaurenoic acid in the gibberellin pathway
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor flux toward bioactive gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early control step suggested; rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in swb9 endosperm; induced under high GA3 treatment; repressed by SWB9
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Influences starch structure and chalkiness through GA homeostasis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: KO2 overexpression increases grain chalkiness and increases short amylopectin chains in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; luciferase assay; EMSA; grain quality phenotyping; amylopectin chain-length analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression reportedly increased in ko2 endosperm, consistent with prior report as cited by authors
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KO2 is directly targeted and repressed by SWB9. 2. KO2 overexpression increases chalkiness and shifts amylopectin chain distribution toward more short chains. 3. KO2 participates with KO1/KOL5 in the SWB9-controlled GA module in endosperm.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for reducing endosperm chalkiness via moderated repression or editing
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCUbi1390 ubiquitin promoter for overexpression; native promoter used in LUC assays

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assigned as an ent-kaurene oxidase-like enzyme contributing to early GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor flux toward bioactive gibberellins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in swb9 endosperm; induced under high GA3 treatment; repressed by SWB9
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Regulates starch synthesis and grain chalkiness via GA levels
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: KOL5 overexpression increases grain chalkiness and short-chain amylopectin proportion; kol5 mutant showed reduced transcript level in endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; luciferase assay; EMSA; grain quality phenotyping; amylopectin chain-length analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct SWB9 promoter target; called KOL5 in text/accession list
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KOL5 is directly bound and repressed by SWB9. 2. KOL5 overexpression increases grain chalkiness and alters amylopectin chain-length distribution. 3. KOL5 functions together with KO1/KO2 in endosperm GA homeostasis relevant to chalkiness.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential endosperm engineering target to reduce chalkiness by limiting excessive GA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pCUbi1390 ubiquitin promoter for overexpression; native promoter used in LUC assays

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Extends glucan chains during amylopectin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly shapes amylopectin structure underlying chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in endosperm; reduced in myb61; promoter activated by MYB61
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Amylopectin chain-length structure associated with grain chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch granule structure and grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced expression under myb61/high GA conditions is associated with increased short amylopectin chains and higher chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter analysis; Y1H; luciferase transactivation; EMSA; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High GA3 reduces MYB61 expression and thereby weakens SSIIa activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated as a direct MYB61 target mediating the chalkiness phenotype, though not independently genetically manipulated here
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB61 directly binds the SSIIa promoter at a GAMYB element. 2. MYB61 activates SSIIa expression in endosperm. 3. Reduced MYB61 under high GA likely lowers SSIIa expression and contributes to abnormal amylopectin structure.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with MYB61/SWB9 for improving amylopectin structure and grain appearance

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces branch points into glucan chains during amylopectin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly shapes amylopectin structure underlying chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Amylopectin chain-length structure associated with grain chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch fine structure and grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MYB61 activation of BEIIb supports normal amylopectin synthesis; reduced activation under high GA is linked to chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter analysis; Y1H; luciferase transactivation; EMSA; RT-qPCR trend in myb61
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High GA3 represses MYB61 and likely weakens BEIIb activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct MYB61 target but no independent genetic validation in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB61 directly binds the BEIIb promoter. 2. MYB61 activates BEIIb transcription. 3. The MYB61-BEIIb link is part of the mechanism connecting GA status to amylopectin structure and chalkiness.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate combinatorial target for grain-quality engineering with upstream regulators

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes inappropriate branch linkages during amylopectin synthesis to shape normal starch structure
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly shapes terminal starch structure linked to chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endosperm-expressed; significantly reduced in myb61; promoter activated by MYB61
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Amylopectin chain-length structure associated with grain chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects starch granule morphology and grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced ISA1 expression in myb61/high GA background is associated with altered amylopectin distribution and increased chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter analysis; Y1H; luciferase transactivation; EMSA; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High GA3 reduces MYB61, thereby weakening ISA1 activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct target of MYB61 in the proposed feedback module
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB61 directly binds and activates ISA1. 2. ISA1 transcript abundance decreases in myb61 endosperm. 3. Reduced ISA1 likely contributes to defective amylopectin structure and chalkiness under high-GA conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream target for improving starch structure when combined with upstream GA-homeostasis genes

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Debranches glucan intermediates to support proper amylopectin assembly
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly shapes terminal starch structure linked to chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endosperm-expressed; significantly reduced in myb61; promoter activated by MYB61
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Amylopectin chain-length structure associated with grain chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch structure and grain quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced PUL expression in myb61/high GA conditions is linked to altered amylopectin structure and increased chalkiness
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter analysis; Y1H; luciferase transactivation; EMSA; RT-qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High GA3 represses MYB61, thereby weakening PUL activation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct MYB61 target within the proposed mechanism
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB61 directly binds and activates PUL. 2. PUL transcript abundance is reduced in myb61 endosperm. 3. The MYB61-PUL connection helps explain how GA feedback alters amylopectin architecture and chalkiness.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream engineering target for improving rice grain appearance quality

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Gibberellin homeostasis and amylopectin biosynthesis in endosperm
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SWB9 transcription; activates SSIIa, BEIIb, ISA1 and PUL transcription; does not bind GBSSI promoter
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in a GA dose-dependent feedback loop: high GA represses MYB61, weakening SWB9 activation and indirectly derepressing KO genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: A crucial upstream regulator linking GA feedback to amylopectin synthesis and grain chalkiness
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Endosperm expression; reduced in myb61 mutant and under high GA3 treatment; increased under 0.1 μM GA3
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Bioactive gibberellins and amylopectin structure determining grain chalkiness
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls starch structure and grain appearance quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: myb61 causes white-belly/chalky endosperm, increased short amylopectin chains, reduced amylose and altered starch physicochemical properties; SWB9 overexpression partially rescues the phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DNA pull-down plus mass spectrometry; Y1H; luciferase transactivation; EMSA; mutant phenotyping; CRISPR editing in Nipponbare; RT-qPCR; genetic rescue by SWB9 overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exogenous GA3 treatment after flowering; developing endosperm stages
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Smaller effect on plant stature than SWB9, but mutation increases grain chalkiness and reduces some grain-quality parameters
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions upstream of SWB9; high GA reduces MYB61 expression in a dose-dependent manner, making MYB61 the key link between GA status and starch biosynthetic gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB61 was identified by DNA pull-down/MS as an SWB9-promoter-binding protein. 2. MYB61 directly activates SWB9 transcription and mutation of MYB61 phenocopies swb9 in endosperm chalkiness. 3. MYB61 directly activates SSIIa, BEIIb, ISA1 and PUL to promote normal amylopectin synthesis. 4. High GA suppresses MYB61 expression, establishing a feedback loop that increases chalkiness when GA is excessive.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for improving rice grain appearance quality by maintaining endosperm GA/starch homeostasis; partial rescue by SWB9 suggests combinatorial engineering or endosperm-specific expression may be effective
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters in Y1H/LUC/EMSA assays; pCUbi1390 ubiquitin promoter for SWB9 overexpression rescue context
